---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Yoon"
date: "2026-03-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(data.table)

ppd_epc_joined <- fread("D:/housing_data/3_clean/clean_data/ppd_epc_joined.csv")

dim(ppd_epc_joined)
names(ppd_epc_joined)
```
```{r}
setDT(ppd_epc_joined)

df_final <- ppd_epc_joined[, .(
  Transaction_ID,
  Price,
  Date_of_Transfer,
  District,
  Property_Type,
  Old_New,
  Duration,
  PPD_Category_Type,
  Record_Status,
  
  CURRENT_ENERGY_RATING,
  CURRENT_ENERGY_EFFICIENCY,
  POTENTIAL_ENERGY_RATING,
  POTENTIAL_ENERGY_EFFICIENCY,
  
  TOTAL_FLOOR_AREA,
  NUMBER_HABITABLE_ROOMS,
  CONSTRUCTION_AGE_BAND,
  BUILT_FORM,
  FLOOR_LEVEL,
  
  Postcode_clean = Postcode_clean.x
)]
```

```{r}
dim(df_final)
```

```{r}
postcode_geo <- fread("D:/housing_data/open_postcode_geo_london.csv")

names(postcode_geo)

head(postcode_geo)
```

```{r}
postcode_geo <- postcode_geo[, .(
  Postcode_clean = V10,
  latitude = V8,
  longitude = V9,
  borough_code = V18,
  borough_name = V19
)]

# make sure postcode format matches your housing data
postcode_geo[, Postcode_clean := toupper(gsub("\\s+", "", Postcode_clean))]

head(postcode_geo)
```

```{r}
df_final <- merge(
  df_final,
  postcode_geo[, .(Postcode_clean, latitude, longitude)],
  by = "Postcode_clean",
  all.x = TRUE
)
```


```{r}
sum(is.na(df_final$latitude))
sum(is.na(df_final$longitude))
head(df_final[, .(Postcode_clean, latitude, longitude)])
```

```{r}
postcode_geo_unique <- postcode_geo[!duplicated(Postcode_clean)]
```


```{r}
df_final[, c("latitude", "longitude") := NULL]
```


```{r}
df_final <- merge(
  df_final,
  postcode_geo_unique[, .(Postcode_clean, latitude, longitude)],
  by = "Postcode_clean",
  all.x = TRUE
)
```


```{r}
nrow(df_final)
```

```{r}
postcode_geo_unique <- postcode_geo[!duplicated(Postcode_clean)]

df_final[, c("latitude", "longitude") := NULL]

df_final <- merge(
  df_final,
  postcode_geo_unique[, .(Postcode_clean, latitude, longitude)],
  by = "Postcode_clean",
  all.x = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
nrow(df_final)
sum(is.na(df_final$latitude))


# total rows
nrow(df_final)

# duplicate transaction IDs
sum(duplicated(df_final$Transaction_ID))

# missing coordinates
sum(is.na(df_final$latitude))
sum(is.na(df_final$longitude))


sum(duplicated(df_final$Postcode_clean))

postcode_counts <- df_final[, .N, by = Postcode_clean][order(-N)]

head(postcode_counts, 20)


sum(duplicated(postcode_geo_unique$Postcode_clean))
```

```{r}
library(data.table)

stations <- fread("D:/housing_data/london_stations.csv")

names(stations)
head(stations)
dim(stations)
```

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(geosphere)

# load station file
stations <- fread("D:/housing_data/london_stations.csv")

# check names first
names(stations)

# ---- CHANGE THESE COLUMN NAMES IF NEEDED ----
# example: rename station file columns to standard names
# replace the left side with your real column names
setnames(
  stations,
  old = c("name", "latitude", "longitude"),
  new = c("station_name", "station_lat", "station_lon"),
  skip_absent = TRUE
)

# keep only needed columns and remove rows with missing coords
stations <- stations[, .(station_name, station_lat, station_lon)]
stations <- stations[!is.na(station_lat) & !is.na(station_lon)]

# make sure numeric
stations[, station_lat := as.numeric(station_lat)]
stations[, station_lon := as.numeric(station_lon)]

# remove any duplicated stations if needed
stations <- unique(stations)

# ---- distance to nearest station ----
station_matrix <- as.matrix(stations[, .(station_lon, station_lat)])

# property coordinate matrix
prop_matrix <- as.matrix(df_final[, .(longitude, latitude)])

# nearest station distance in km
dist_mat <- distm(prop_matrix, station_matrix, fun = distHaversine)

df_final[, dist_to_station_km := apply(dist_mat, 1, min) / 1000]

# optional: nearest station name too
nearest_idx <- max.col(-dist_mat, ties.method = "first")
df_final[, nearest_station := stations$station_name[nearest_idx]]

# checks
summary(df_final$dist_to_station_km)
sum(is.na(df_final$dist_to_station_km))

# save
saveRDS(df_final, "D:/housing_data/3_clean/ppd_epc_geo_station_joined.rds")
fwrite(df_final, "D:/housing_data/3_clean/ppd_epc_geo_station_joined.csv")
```



```{r}

```
